items.title: Split decision as Gina Rinehart, Wright family both claim legal wins

children.content: Gina Rinehart is dealt a blow, with a court finding the family of her father's former business partner has the right to potentially hundreds of millions of dollars in royalties from lucrative Pilbara mining assets.

gallery.altText: A woman in a black jacket and pink shirt steps on stage

thumbnail.altText: A woman in a black jacket and pink shirt steps on stage

items.title: Here's what you need to know about today's Gina Rinehart court ruling

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

children.content: Today's blockbuster court ruling over mining tenements worth billions of dollars in WA's iron ore rich Pilbara is the culmination of a complicated legal battle spanning more than a decade that has pitted family against family and siblings against each other.

items.title: Federal Court allows appeal by lesbian group seeking to ban trans women

children.content: The Federal Court allows a lesbian action group's appeal to be allowed to legally exclude transgender women from its public events.

image.altText: Lesbian Action Group celebrate the win outside court

items.title: Albanese, Sultan of Brunei share 'deep concern' amid ongoing energy crisis

children.content: Brunei may be able to help Australia weather the economic chaos caused by the Iran war by sending additional supplies of fertiliser, the prime minister says.

image.altText: Two men in suits holding booklets stand in front of gold chairs.

items.title: Taylor's migration plan doesn't make much sense. It doesn't have to

children.content: More than a day after Angus Taylor unveiled the first plank of the Coalition's long-awaited migration policy and many media interviews later, it's still unclear what the opposition is actually proposing to change.

image.altText: A man in a suit extends his hand in exclamation while speaking to a group of journalists.

avatar.alt: A headshot of a woman against a grey background

items.title: Strait of Hormuz blockade could turn into global agrifood crisis

children.content: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations says ships in the Strait of Hormuz need to start moving again to avoid a global agrifood crisis. Here's why.

image.altText: Green wheat in focus in the foreground with blurred mature wheat in the background

images.altText: a us navy destroyer ship moving right to left in the ocean

articles.title: Peace talks, strait closed, China calls out US blockade: What to know today

children.content: One week into the ceasefire, two sets of peace talks have taken place but the only commitment is to talk more.

image.altText: ABC reporter Matthew Doran standing in front of the Riyadh skyline.

articles.title: King Charles to have tea with Donald Trump during US trip

children.content: King Charles will have tea and a private meeting with US President Donald Trump during his US state visit later this month, with trips to Washington, New York and Virginia.

image.altText: Two men in smart suits stand side by side, one raises his right hand to his head. Two women wear smart, fashionable hats.

children.content: The talks come as both the US and Iran continue to impose their own separate restrictions on the flow of vessels in and out of the Strait of Hormuz. Follow live.

updates.title: 'The context of this visit is energy security': Penny Wong
Anthony Albanese is speaking from Brunei
Trump not thinking about extending two-week ceasefire

items.title: Police investigating claim Katy Perry sexually assaulted Ruby Rose

children.content: Melbourne-born actress Ruby Rose claims US popstar Katy Perry assaulted her at a nightclub in 2010.

image.altText: Ruby Rose and Katy Perry

items.title: Hotel owner looks to sue Australia for $11m over unpaid refugee bills

children.content: A hotel in Papua New Guinea is attempting to sue the Australian government over accommodation costs for refugees previously detained on Manus Island.

image.altText: A Papua New Guinean man with a moustache sits in a chair wearing a button up shirt.

items.title: Teen admits creating deepfakes in Australian-first prosecution

children.content: An Adelaide teenager who was the first person charged under new nationwide laws aimed at tackling adult deepfake pornography has pleaded guilty to two counts of creating or altering sexual material without consent.

image.altText: William Yeates is wearing a white shirt and looking over his shoulder

items.title: Media boss's wife feared she would die, tore his clothing during alleged attack, court told

children.content: The wife of media executive Antony Catalano fled the couple's luxury apartment and flagged down a passing car for help following a sustained attack by her husband that caused hospitalisation, police allege.

image.altText: A side profile shot of Australian Community Media executive chairman Antony Catalano in an outdoor setting.

items.title: Duke of Sussex honours Indigenous veterans in Canberra visit

children.content: The Duke of Sussex is in the nation's capital without his wife Meghan and will speak at an Invictus Australia event.

gallery.altText: Prince Harry at the Australian War Memorial

thumbnail.altText: Prince Harry at the Australian War Memorial
Prince Harry lays a wreath at the Australian War Memorial
Crowd at War Memorial for Prince Harry visit

items.title: Most truckers fear they won't last 12 months, industry survey says

children.content: Trucking industry peak group NatRoad is calling on the federal government to urgently implement the $1 billion Economic Resilience Program it promised two weeks ago.

image.altText: A large blue truck with cattle transporting trailers attached in a small country town.

items.title: Reasons released for Butters AFL Tribunal decision

children.content: The AFL Tribunal releases its reasons for finding Zak Butters guilty of abusive and insulting language to umpire Nick Foot, saying it was "satisfied to the requisite standard" that he had made the remarks and that it was "implausible" that the umpire would have made it up.

image.altText: A Port Adelaide AFL player (middle) stands in the rain staring up field with a concerned look on his face.

items.title: How the technology we use every day is changing our brains

children.content: If it feels harder to concentrate than it used to, it's not just you. Scientists say your brain is reacting exactly as it was designed to — but in a very different world.

gallery.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of an abstract human brain, using coloured paper cut-outs against a blue paper background.

items.title: Which shade of white is right? Dentists explain the teeth colours

children.content: Experts say there are many factors that influence how white a tooth appears, and it's not just about whitening or what we’re eating.

image.altText: A close up image of a woman looking at her teeth in a small mirror

items.title: How The Babadook inspired the 'scariest movie you'll ever hear'

children.content: Filmed in director Ian Tuason's childhood home and based on his own experiences caring for his dying parents, Undertone has become a sleeper hit.

image.altText: A woman in a dimly lit room screams

items.title: Brothers living in squalor missed multiple health appointments, report finds

children.content: A 48-page report has been released on the interactions between health care workers and two siblings who were discovered living in squalor in May 2020.

items.title: Exposure of Australia's fertiliser deficit could inspire manufacturing comeback

children.content: Projects are in the works to expand Australia's domestic fertiliser supply as prices rise by 60 per cent.

image.altText: A hand holds brilliant white fertiliser while a truck tips it off in the background

items.title: Experts tip further flight disruptions as Virgin, Qantas trim domestic capacity

children.content: Australian airlines are halting entire routes and merging slots to ensure planes are full, as the aviation industry battles soaring jet fuel costs and likely supply uncertainty over the coming months.

image.altText: A virgin plane taking off into the sky.

items.title: 'Calculated' theft of $12,000 worth of fuel prompts arrest

children.content: A 36-year-old man has been charged with theft amid an investigation into as many as 20 petrol drive-offs, which police say involved an SUV that contained a tank used to carry stolen diesel.

image.altText: The back of a car with its boot open.

items.title: Man cleared of stalking Israeli President Herzog during Sydney trip

children.content: Rajab Ali was arrested in February while President Isaac Herzog was in Sydney to pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi Beach terror attack.

image.altText: A man with a moustache and beard looking at the camera inside a room.

items.title: What a 'super El Niño' would mean for Australia's weather in 2026

children.content: Australia's weather is expected to shift rapidly thanks to the probable emergence of an incoming El Niño, meaning drought, extreme heat and bushfires are on the way.

articles.title: Drought fuels biggest cattle sell-off in 20 years, as El Niño looms

children.content: More than 500,000 head of cattle have already been sold across New South Wales this year, with Scone seeing its biggest sale in 20 years.

items.title: Workers at embattled smelter face last pay check amid calls for certainty

children.content: As what could be their last payday looms, an alliance of unions says more should be done to ensure stability for 200 workers at Australia's only manganese smelter.

image.altText: A steel worker in highly protective gear working on hot steel products.

items.title: Fatal kick accused committed to stand trial

children.content: The man accused of delivering the blow that later killed a 22-year-old on the Gold Coast has been committed to stand trial.

image.altText: A man in a suit walks with young men around him outside a court.

items.title: Finance giant fined millions for sending unstoppable spam

children.content: Latitude Financial has been fined millions of dollars for a repeated breach of spam laws, including promoting credit cards without giving recipients a way to opt-out.

image.altText: A Latitude Financial logo advertising interest free credit.

items.title: High Court throws out Victoria's $5,000 cap on political donations

children.content: Victoria's political donation and disclosure laws have been scrapped by the High Court just seven months out from the state's upcoming election.

image.altText: A square building behind a sign saying "High Court of Australia".

items.title: Tougher bail laws creating 'perverse incentive' for children to plead guilty

children.content: Several lawyers have told a state parliament inquiry into the youth justice system that bail laws are encouraging children to plead guilty to crimes they may not have committed.

image.altText: A person in shadow walks through a caged internal courtyard covered in more shadows.

items.title: Gold mine stops underground operations after magnitude-4.5 earthquake

children.content: All underground workers at Newmont's Cadia gold mine have been brought to the surface as inspections are carried out following an earthquake.

image.altText: Satellite map image with yellow dots of earthquake locations..

items.title: New images released with Hobart stadium precinct master plan

children.content: A master plan for the precinct surrounding the future AFL stadium in Hobart has been unveiled, while the ABC understands the company behind New Zealand's premier stadium in Christchurch is among those competing to build it.

gallery.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial view od Hobart's waterfront, with an artist's impression of the stadium and new housing.

items.title: Kanye West postpones Marseille concert after France considers banning him

children.content: US rapper Kanye West has postponed his upcoming concert in the French city of Marseille, following reports that the country's interior minister was seeking to block the performance.

image.altText: A man with very short hair and goatee beard, wears a black top and gold neck chain.

items.title: Typhoon Sinlaku batters Northern Mariana Islands and Guam

children.content: A super typhoon with ferocious winds and heavy rains made landfall in the Commonwealth of Northern Mariana Islands (CNMI) overnight, destroying homes and infrastructure.

image.altText: Cars sit submerged in water along streets filled with dark brown water under a dark grey sky

items.title: New footage captures moment Artemis II crew welcomed home

children.content: New footage shows the moment a recovery crew opens the hatch of Artemis II's Orion capsule and welcomes its crew members home after their mission around the Moon.

image.altText: Crew opening capsule.

gallery.altText: Two astronauts in orange space suits are seen sitting in a space capsule. One of them is giving a thumbs up sign.

thumbnail.altText: Two astronauts in orange space suits are seen sitting in a space capsule. One of them is giving a thumbs up sign.
